text.title: Laravel PHPUnit / Pest Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to scaffold a Laravel test suite end-to-end.

step.name: scaffold-suite
step.action: Create `tests/Feature/` + `tests/Unit/` directories. Configure `phpunit.xml`: connection to real test DB, coverage report, suites. Install Pest if not present.

step.name: seed-factories-and-fakes
step.action: Ensure every model has a factory under `database/factories/`. In each Feature test class call `Mail::fake()`, `Queue::fake()`, `Event::fake()` in `setUp` (Pest: `beforeEach`).
step.decision-gate: If a fake is created AFTER the action under test, BLOCK per `fakes-before-action` rule.

step.name: author-feature-tests
step.action: For each endpoint write happy + sad paths via `postJson` / `getJson`. Assert status + values via `assertJsonPath`. Use `RefreshDatabase` on every Feature test that touches DB.
step.decision-gate: If `assertJsonStructure` used as value check, BLOCK per `assertjsonpath-not-structure` rule.

step.name: author-unit-tests
step.action: Write Pest / PHPUnit unit tests under `tests/Unit/`. No Laravel boot — instantiate the SUT directly. Mock collaborators with Mockery if needed.

step.name: wire-coverage-gate
step.action: Run `pest --coverage --min=80` (or PHPUnit equivalent) in CI. Add a separate Pitest-like mutation job for payment / auth modules.
step.decision-gate: If threshold lowered as "quick fix", BLOCK per `coverage-gate-in-ci` rule.
